items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` **Triggered by:** PR #57924 by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed PR #57924 added `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/client-options.md` ŌĆö a new reference document describing all supported `@`@clientOption`` keys for the Azure management-plane C# emitter. The document covers two decorator keys: - `resource-rbac-roles`: defines RBAC role definitions for provisioning code generation - `resource-name-constraint`: overrides resource name length/pattern constraints when spec decorators can't be used without causing breaking changes The emitter source files (`resource-detection.ts`, `resource-metadata.ts`) were updated to read the new `resource-name-constraint` client option. ## Gaps Found - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/index.mdx` has no link to the new `client-options.md` file ŌĆö users browsing the docs hub will not know it exists - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` has no mention of `@`@clientOption`` support or a pointer to `client-options.md` - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/emitter.md` also has no cross-reference to the new `@`@clientOption`` documentation, even though `@`@clientOption`` keys directly influence resource code generation driven by the emitter
­¤ōÉ Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files #### 1a. Edit `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/index.mdx` **Edit** this file to add a link to the new `client-options.md` alongside the existing emitter link. Replace the existing `## Emitter usage` section footer: ```mdx ## Emitter usage [See documentation](./emitter.md) ``` with: ```mdx ## Emitter usage [See documentation](./emitter.md) ## Supported `@`@clientOption`` Keys The emitter recognizes several `@`@clientOption`` keys that provide additional metadata for resource code generation. [See documentation](./client-options.md) ``` #### 1b. Edit `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` **Edit** the README to add a reference to the `@`@clientOption`` doc. Append a new section after the emitter options block (before the end of file): ```markdown ## Supported `@`@clientOption`` keys The emitter recognizes several `@`@clientOption`` keys (scoped to `"csharp"`) that allow spec authors to provide additional metadata driving resource code generation ŌĆö for example RBAC role definitions and resource name constraints. [See the full reference](docs/client-options.md) ``` #### 1c. Edit `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/emitter.md` **Edit** this file to add a cross-reference at the end, after all the emitter option entries: ```markdown ## Supported `@`@clientOption`` keys In addition to emitter options, the management emitter reads per-resource metadata from `@`@clientOption`` decorators in the TypeSpec spec. [See full `@`@clientOption`` reference](./client-options.md) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No snippet changes are required ŌĆö this is a documentation-only update with no new test-backed code samples. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The `README.md` for this emitter package is a tool/generator README (not a client SDK README), so the standard Getting Started / Key Concepts / Examples / Troubleshooting / Next Steps / Contributing template does not apply directly. The current structure (Install ŌåÆ Emitter usage ŌåÆ Emitter options) is appropriate. The only gap is the missing `@`@clientOption`` reference section added in Step 1b above. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt && npm install` 2. `npm run lint` ŌĆö verify no linting errors in emitter source 3. Manually verify that the markdown links resolve correctly (e.g., `./client-options.md` from `index.mdx` and `emitter.md`, and `docs/client-options.md` from `README.md`) No dotnet build, test, or snippet/API export steps are needed for this documentation-only change.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [Mgmt Generator] Docs: Link new client-options.md from index and README
